step_by_step_reasoning: The architecture shows a multi-account CI/CD pipeline using AWS Control Tower to govern multiple AWS environments (CI/CD, Playground, Development, and Production). Engineers push code to GitHub, which triggers Tekton pipelines running on Amazon EKS. Built images are stored in Amazon ECR. Cross-account promotions to target EKS clusters in Playground, Development, and Production are secured via IAM Roles. Amazon Security Lake (omitted as not in standard service list) centralizes environment logs.

graph.name: SwiftX Secure Multi-Account CI/CD Pipeline
graph.notes: An automated and secure CI/CD and deployment pipeline across multiple AWS accounts managed by AWS Control Tower, targeting EKS clusters.

nodes.name: Engineers
nodes.notes: Pushes code changes and opens pull requests.

nodes.name: GitHub
nodes.notes: Hosts application code and triggers CI/CD workflows.

nodes.name: Amazon EKS (CI/CD)
nodes.notes: Runs Tekton pipelines, SonarQube, HashiCorp Vault, and Buildah for image assembly.

nodes.name: IAM Role
nodes.notes: Enables cross-account access and promotion of ECR images.

nodes.name: AWS Control Tower
nodes.notes: Provisions and governs multiple segregated AWS accounts.

nodes.name: Amazon EKS (Target Environments)
nodes.notes: Hosts ephemeral, dev, and production application workloads.

edges.notes: Deploys images to target EKS clusters.
